summary:
Mesio-occlusal rests are preferred over other types due to their optimal force distribution which enhances mechanical efficiency and reduces risk of dislocation by strategically attaching at critical sites along the abutment teeth. This approach ensures better support while maintaining high levels of functionality necessary during mastication processes involving these clasped structures.

The summary captures only two core elements from your original response related directly back to answering "Bar-clasp assembly most commonly uses what type?" First, it highlights preference towards 'mesio-occlusal' as opposed to alternatives like ‘disto’ or ‘buccal’. Second, it explains why this choice matters clinically – specifically regarding improved function through optimized load bearing capabilities alongside reduced potential complications such as misalignment issues associated with improper placement strategies mentioned earlier but not included here since they were deemed extraneous details according to instructions given initially." Based strictly upon those guidelines you specified previously reiterating focus solely within required limits without additional commentary beyond essential content pertinent justifying use case scenario presented above,"
To summarize concisely based exactly per initial instruction parameters now applied correctly :

Mesio-occlusal rests enhance bar clasp effectiveness via superior force dispersion; hence they're predominantly utilized compared to alternative positions because they maximize structural integrity & minimize movement concerns crucially impacting dental prosthetic success rates significantly more positively when properly placed near specific anatomically favorable locations relative to individual tooth configurations involved herein described contextually appropriately limited scope application."
